name: Fighter: Protection Fighting Style Reaction
description: A level 3 Fighter (Bram) with the Protection fighting style and a shield stands adjacent to a Wizard ally (Elara). A Hobgoblin attacks Elara. Server raises a 'protection' reaction opportunity on Bram; player accepts; attacker re-rolls the d20 taking the lower value (normal mode: min(original, reroll)); reroll forces a miss. Asserts exact HP (unchanged) and that the reaction flow completed end-to-end. BUG-FS-1 is RESOLVED — the hasProtectionStyle / hasShieldEquipped flags are now propagated from buildCombatResources() into combatant.resources via initiative-handler.ts::assembleCombatantResources().

monsters.comment: Hobgoblin positioned 5 ft from Elara, 5 ft diagonal from Bram so Bram is within Protection's 5 ft ally-scan reach of Elara.
monsters.name: Hobgoblin Soldier

actions.comment: High initiative so PCs go first.
Force the Hobgoblin to target Elara on its upcoming turn — ensures Bram is the ally-scan protector (adjacent to Elara) and rules out the AI deciding to attack Bram instead.

Queue the two server-side d20 rolls for the Hobgoblin's upcoming attack: [18, 2]. Slot 1 (18): initial attack d20 -> 18+5=23 vs AC 12 -> HIT. Slot 2 (2): Protection reroll d20 -> normal rollMode -> min(18,2)=2 -> 2+5=7 vs AC 12 -> MISS. No damage rolled (miss), no further queue entries needed.

input.label: monster attack d20=18 (hit AC 12) + protection reroll d20=2 (miss AC 12)

actions.comment: Elara ends turn. Server processes the Hobgoblin's AI turn: d20=18 -> hit -> initiateAttack -> Protection opportunity raised on Bram -> AI pauses awaiting reaction.

Poll /reactions until the Protection opportunity for Bram is exposed. This PROVES BUG-FS-1 is fixed: before the fix, no reaction was ever offered because hasProtectionStyle / hasShieldEquipped were not propagated into combatant.resources.
Accept Protection. Server consumes a d20 from the queue (2), applies normal-mode rule min(18,2)=2 -> attack total 7 -> miss. Marks Bram.resources.reactionUsed=true. Emits ProtectionApplied event.

After the Hobgoblin's turn completes (miss -> no damage), Elara's HP is exactly unchanged at 20. Without Protection the Hobgoblin would have dealt 1d8+3 damage (4-11) dropping her to 9-16 HP -- so exact 20 is a strong, unambiguous assertion that the Protection reroll actually happened.
Bram untouched (Hobgoblin targeted Elara). HP=28 confirms the attack did not spill over to Bram.
Advance to round 2 Bram turn (reactionUsed auto-resets at turn start). Reaching this step confirms the attack-reaction pipeline cleanly completed after Protection fired.
